title: Strings
summary: Read and transform text without changing the original value.
refresher: Strings are immutable sequences with zero-based indexes. `.length` counts characters, and template literals insert expressions with `${...}`.

blocks.body: Strings are immutable sequences with zero-based indexes. `.length` counts characters, and template literals insert expressions with `${...}`. Read and transform text without changing the original value.

A string behaves like a sequence you can read but not edit in place. Indexing retrieves one character, and methods create new string values. The original stays unchanged until you assign a returned value back to a variable. Template literals are useful when a message mixes text with calculated values.

blocks.caption: Index zero reads the first character, while slice stops before its ending index.
Keep the transformed string returned by the method.

blocks.body: In the corrected version, the method first creates `"CS"`. Assignment then stores that new string in `major`. For slicing, say the indexes aloud: start is included, end is excluded, so `slice(1, 4)` reads positions one, two, and three.

blocks.title: Watch this detail
blocks.body: A string with length five ends at index four. Reading `text[text.length]` goes one place too far.

blocks.why: Slice includes positions zero through two and excludes position three.
The method returns a new string without changing the value in label.
